name: Beginner Bread
summary: A forgiving first sourdough loaf with overnight proof and a Dutch-oven bake.
tips: If your dough is ready for shaping when it's puffy and slightly jiggly, not sticky and collapsing.
If you're short on time, skip the overnight proof and bake after the 1–2 hour second rise.
Don't skip the cooling! Cutting too soon will ruin the crumb texture.
Want a softer crust? Wrap the warm bread in a kitchen towel after cooling 15 minutes.

steps.title: Mix the Dough
steps.instruction: In a large bowl, mix the starter and water until mostly dissolved. Add flour and stir until a shaggy dough forms. Cover and let rest (autolyse) for 30–45 minutes.

steps.title: Add Salt
steps.instruction: Sprinkle salt over the dough and incorporate by hand, folding and gently pressing until fully combined.

steps.title: Stretch & Folds
steps.instruction: During roughly the first 2 hours, perform 2–3 rounds of stretch and folds — one set every 30–45 minutes — to build strength. The timer counts each interval; reset it after every fold.
steps.tip: A set is a few folds around the bowl; the dough gets smoother and stronger each time.

steps.title: Bulk Fermentation
steps.instruction: After your folds, let the dough continue at room temperature until it looks puffy and has risen by about half. Counting from mixing, that is roughly 4–6 hours total.

steps.title: Pre-shape
steps.instruction: Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Gently shape it into a round and let it rest, uncovered, for 20–30 minutes.

steps.title: Final Shaping
steps.instruction: Reshape the dough into a tight ball or oval. Place it seam-side up in a floured proofing basket or bowl lined with a floured cloth.
steps.tip: The dough is ready for shaping when it's puffy and slightly jiggly, not sticky.

steps.title: Final Proof
steps.instruction: Cover and let rise at room temperature for 1–2 hours, or refrigerate overnight (8–12 hours) for improved flavor and structure.
steps.tip: Short on time? The room-temp proof is fine. For best flavor, use the overnight fridge proof.

steps.title: Preheat Oven
steps.instruction: Place a Dutch oven in your oven and preheat to 475°F (245°C) for at least 30 minutes before baking.

steps.title: Score and Bake
steps.instruction: Invert the dough onto parchment, score the top with a sharp blade, and carefully transfer it to the hot Dutch oven. Bake covered for 20–25 minutes, then remove the lid and bake 20–25 minutes uncovered, until deep golden brown.

steps.title: Bake Uncovered
steps.instruction: Remove the lid and continue baking uncovered for 20–25 minutes until deep golden brown.

steps.title: Cool
steps.instruction: Transfer the baked loaf to a wire rack and cool at least 1 hour before slicing.
steps.tip: Don't skip cooling — cutting too soon ruins the crumb. For a softer crust, wrap the warm loaf in a towel after 15 minutes.
